title: -Contract No. 32500124 Amendment: Buhler Commercial for FY 2023 Retaining Wall and Storm Drain Improvement Project

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an amendment to Contract
No. 32500124 with Buhler Commercial for work on the FY 2023 Retaining Wall and
Storm Drain Improvement Project, Specification Nos. 23-11616-C & 23-11614-C,
increasing the contract amount by $250,000 for an amended total not to exceed
$2,224,009.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for this contract amendment is available in the State Transportation Tax Fund
(Fund 127).
State Transportation Tax (Fund 127) .......................................................$250,000
Total construction amendment amount $250,000
No other funding is required, and no other projects will be delayed due to this expenditure.
Original Authorized Contract Amount (NTE) .........................................$1,974,009
This Amendment Amount ........................................................................$250,000
Amended Contract amount (New NTE) $2,224,009
sections.current_situation:
The FY 2023 Retaining Wall and Storm Drain Improvement Project (the Project),
Specification Nos. 23-11616-C & 23-11614-C, is currently under construction by Buhler
Commercial. The Project is approximately 75 percent complete but has encountered
unforeseen conditions that require additional work and funding.
Two significant site conditions were discovered during construction that were not
apparent during the design phase:
 Subsurface groundwater was encountered at a shallower depth than anticipated
during drilling of the cast-in-place pier foundations along Hilgard Avenue. The

presence of groundwater required the contractor to implement temporary steel
casings to maintain hole stability, prevent soil caving, and safely complete the
drilled pier installations. Although construction of the retaining wall has been
successfully completed, the additional casing and drilling measures required to
address the unexpected groundwater condition resulted in a substantial increase
in construction costs.
 During excavation for the Upper Columbia Path staircase installation, the
contractor exposed portions of an irregular rock outcrop that were not fully visible
during design investigations. Once uncovered, it became clear that adjustments
to the staircase profile were required to ensure a stable and constructible
installation that accounts for existing geological features. These refinements
reflect appropriate adjustments based on field-verified site conditions. This work
is currently on hold, pending the Council approval of this contract amendment.
The Project requires $250,000 in additional funds to cover the costs associated with

sections.background:
On July 23, 2024, Council authorized Contract No. 32500124 with Buhler Commercial,
the lowest responsive and responsible bidder, to construct the FY 2023 Retaining Wall
and Storm Drain Improvement Project for a not-to-exceed amount of $1,974,009, which
included a 10% contingency.
The Project includes several infrastructure repairs and improvements throughout
various locations in the City, including storm drain maintenance hole upgrades, retaining
wall replacements on Hilgard Avenue and La Loma Avenue, bridge abutment repairs at
La Loma Bridge, pedestrian safety improvements at the Piedmont Avenue and
Channing Way traffic circle, a new staircase at Upper Columbia Path, traffic calming
improvements at the Woolsey Street and Eton Avenue intersection, and repairs to five
existing concrete paths.
Prior to construction, the design team completed extensive due-diligence investigations
at the project sites, including utility verification and exploratory borings to assess soil
characteristics and the potential for groundwater. While these efforts provided critical
baseline information, certain subsurface and geologic features such as the
unexpectedly shallow groundwater encountered at Hilgard Avenue and the detailed
configuration of the rock outcrop at the Upper Columbia Path staircase, could only be
fully understood once excavation began. These field-verified conditions necessitated
refinements to the planned construction approach.
Page 2
Page 3 of 4
Contract No. 32500124: Buhler Commercial CONSENT CALENDAR
for FY 2023 Retaining Wall and Storm Drain Improvement Project January 27, 2026
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
This contract amendment will help ensure the successful completion of infrastructure
repair and pedestrian improvement projects. The retaining wall and pathway
improvements support the City's commitment to maintaining safe, accessible
infrastructure that encourages walking and reduces reliance on vehicle transportation.
sections.rationale:
The additional funding is necessary to address critical safety and structural
requirements that emerged during construction and could not have been anticipated
during the design phase.
sections.alternatives:
No alternative action was considered for this project. If the proposed contract
amendment is not approved, the completion of these infrastructure improvements would
be delayed, potentially creating safety hazards at the construction sites. The state
funding is readily available for project completion in the appropriate fund, and is meant
to cover this exact type of situation.
CONTACT PERSON
Wahid Amiri, Deputy Director, Public Works, (510) 981-6396
Terrence Salonga, Interim Supervising Civil Engineer, Department of Public Works, (510)
981-6447
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32500124 AMENDMENT: BUHLER COMMERCIAL FOR FY 2023
RETAINING WALL AND STORM DRAIN IMPROVEMENT PROJECT
WHEREAS, on July 23, 2024, by Resolution No. 71,455-N.S., the City Council authorized
Contract No. 32500124 with Buhler Commercial for the FY 2023 Retaining Wall and
Storm Drain Improvement Project, Specification Nos. 23-11616-C & 23-11614-C, in an
amount not to exceed $1,974,009; and
WHEREAS, the City has neither the labor nor the equipment necessary to undertake this
FY 2023 Retaining Wall and Storm Drain Improvement Project; and
WHEREAS, unforeseen conditions were encountered during construction, including the
discovery of groundwater during the installation of the Hilgard Avenue retaining wall piers
requiring temporary steel casings and additional drilling to prevent soil collapse; and
WHEREAS, field conditions at the Upper Columbia Path staircase revealed variations in
the existing rock outcrop that required design modifications during construction to ensure
proper integration with existing terrain; and
WHEREAS, this Contract has insufficient budget to complete the aforementioned
additional work required to address these unforeseen site conditions; and
WHEREAS, funding of $250,000 is available in the State Transportation Tax Fund (Fund
127).
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager is authorized to execute an amendment to Contract No. 32500124 with
Buhler Commercial for work on the FY 2023 Retaining Wall and Storm Drain Improvement
Project, Specification Nos. 23-11616-C & 23-11614-C, increasing the contract amount by
$250,000 for an amended total not to exceed $2,224,009. A record signature copy of said
contract and any amendments will be on file in the Office of the City Clerk.
